summary: Wire runtime-control execution, retention, and artifact policy.
rationale: This slice connects the existing workflow runtime adapter to durable runtime-control events, executor leases, resume handling, and artifact retention. It intentionally touches the runtime orchestrator boundary plus runtime-control package code so the runtime can emit DB-first progress while preserving the orchestrator as the concrete execution adapter.
line_count_rationale: The orchestrator is a pre-existing oversized runtime adapter; this PR adds only the small runtime-control event sink and checkpoint integration needed for DB-first execution, with focused runtime-control tests and full pytest verification.
deletion_targets: runtime progress callbacks that are not persisted as runtime-control events
executor resume paths that lose canonical checkpoint failure semantics
artifact output modes that cannot distinguish prod-minimal from dev/debug retention
risks: WorkflowRuntime must remain the only runtime-control module allowed to import the concrete orchestrator adapter.
Artifact policy changes must not expose provider payloads, cookies, auth headers, browser storage, or raw resume text outside the configured visibility policy.
Retention cleanup must not delete active runtime-control audit state or executor leases.
